title: Town of Blacksburg Home Occupation Use and Design Standards
excerpt: Home occupation standards restrict outside storage and hazardous materials, limit home occupation floor area to 25% of the dwelling, allow one nonresident worker, prohibit on-site advertising signs, and require traffic volumes consistent with nearby dwellings without adding parking spaces during the home occupation.
full_text: (a) Intent—Under certain unique circumstances a small-scaled commercial activity may be an appropriate use within a residential dwelling. The standards and procedure for establishing such uses are intended to limit the scope and nature of such uses and ensure compatibility with the adjoining properties. (b) General standards: (1) Home occupations shall be clearly incidental and secondary to the use of the dwelling for residential purposes and shall not change its character as a residence. The use of colors, materials, construction, lighting, or other means inconsistent with a residential use shall be prohibited. (2) No equipment or process shall be used in a home occupation which creates noise in excess of 60 dB(A) measured at the property line, or vibration, glare, fumes, odors, or electrical interference detectable to the normal senses off the premises or through common walls. In the case of electrical interference, no equipment or process shall be used which creates visual or audible interference in any radio or television receivers off the premises or through common walls. (3) There shall be no outside storage of goods, products, equipment, or other materials associated with the home occupation, nor shall these materials be stored inside an accessory structure. No toxic, explosive, flammable, radioactive, or other hazardous materials used in conjunction with the home occupation shall be used, sold, or stored on the site. (4) The maximum floor area permitted for a home occupation shall be twenty-five (25) percent of the finished floor area of the dwelling unit. Storage of goods or products shall not exceed five (5) percent of the finished floor area. (5) One (1) person who is not a permanent resident of the dwelling may be engaged or employed in the home occupation. (6) The street address of the home occupation may be used in advertisements. (7) No sign may be placed on the property advertising the home occupation. (8) The type and volume of traffic generated by a home occupation shall be consistent with the traffic generation characteristics of other dwellings in the area. In addition, the lot or property on which the home occupation is conducted shall not have any parking spaces added to it during the time the home occupation is being conducted. (9) The home occupation shall not increase demand on water, sewer, or garbage collection services to the extent that the combined demand for the dwelling and home occupation is significantly more than is normal to the use of the property for residential purposes.

title: Town of Blacksburg Zoning Guidance for Business Licenses and Home Occupations
excerpt: The Town instructs prospective businesses to confirm that zoning allows the proposed business use before applying for a business license. The Town's zoning page states that home occupations, meaning businesses run administratively out of the home, are allowed in every Blacksburg zoning district subject to the Home Occupation Application ordinance standards.

title: Blacksburg Zoning Ordinance Off-Street Parking Schedule for Home Occupations
excerpt: The Blacksburg Zoning Ordinance off-street parking schedule lists Home occupation under residential use types and points minimum parking review to section 4211(B)(8), with no maximum parking standard stated in the schedule.
full_text: (a) When a building includes a combination of uses as set forth in this Division the required parking will be the sum of the required parking for each use. (b) Where the parking requirement for a particular use is not defined in this Division, and where no similar use is listed, the Administrator shall determine the number of spaces to be provided based on requirements for similar uses, location of the proposed use, expected demand and traffic generated by the proposed use, and appropriate traffic engineering and planning criteria and information. (c) All references to square feet (sq. ft.) in the parking requirements below shall mean the square feet of gross floor area, unless specifically stated otherwise. (d) All references to maximum occupancy shall mean the maximum occupancy as determined pursuant to the Virginia Uniform Statewide Building Code. (e) Where a fractional space results during calculation of required parking, the determination of whether to round the required number of parking spaces up or down to the next whole number shall be at the discretion of the applicant. (Ord. No. 1598, § 1, 2-8-11)

title: Town of Blacksburg Planning and Building Department Building Safety Review
excerpt: The Building Safety Division reviews construction plans for conformance with the Virginia Uniform Statewide Building Code and town ordinances, issues construction permits, inspects projects for approved-plan compliance, and issues certificates of occupancy.

title: Virginia Department of Health Food Establishment Permit Guidance
excerpt: VDH states that foodservice operators opening a new or remodeled foodservice facility should submit plan review materials to the local health department, apply for a Food Establishment Permit before opening, and request a health department inspection before the permit is issued.

title: VDACS Home and Commercial Kitchen-Based Food Businesses
excerpt: VDACS explains that food establishments, including private homes that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food for sale, are subject to Virginia food laws and related regulations unless an exception applies, and operating generally requires inspection and a permit.

title: Montgomery County Home Occupation Zoning Guidance
excerpt: Montgomery County states that home occupations in the unincorporated county are regulated by the zoning ordinance, are permitted in listed residential and agricultural districts, must be secondary to the residence, may not exceed 25% of dwelling floor area, may use an accessory building, and must avoid exterior impacts such as traffic, parking demand, noise, vibration, glare, odors, or electrical interference.

title: Montgomery County Home Occupation Health, Inspections, and Sign Review Triggers
excerpt: The Montgomery County home occupation form directs food-preparation businesses to contact the Health Department about a food service permit, directs projects with additions or structural alterations to contact Montgomery County Inspections, and notes that a home occupation sign requires sign-requirement verification with Planning and a separate permit.

title: Montgomery County Planning and GIS Zoning Permit Review Sequence
excerpt: Montgomery County Planning and GIS Services administers zoning and subdivision ordinances in unincorporated areas. The county directs applicants to submit zoning permit materials to Planning and GIS Services before applying for a building permit at the Department of Inspections, with Planning and GIS listed for zoning questions and Building Inspections listed for building permit questions.
section_ref: Planning and GIS Services; zoning permit approval steps

title: Montgomery County GIS Zoning Map and Parcel Lookup
excerpt: Montgomery County GIS identifies MyGIS OneView as the county's interactive public map resource for parcel, land, and location information, lists a zoning map in the map gallery, and cautions that maps and GIS data are reference materials rather than legal determinations of official county records.
section_ref: GIS and Mapping Services; Map Gallery and disclaimer

title: Montgomery County Residential Building Permit and USBC Compliance
excerpt: Montgomery County's residential building permit application says a permit must be issued before construction begins, applications must include construction plans, all building work must comply with the Virginia Uniform Statewide Building Code, and applicants must contact Planning and GIS Services to discuss zoning and development-code compliance.
section_ref: Residential Building Permit Application; updated April 2025

title: New River Health District Food Establishment Plan Review and Permits
excerpt: The Virginia Department of Health New River Health District describes environmental health inspections for restaurants and other establishments, identifies establishment plan review and permit fees, and provides applications for new or remodeled food establishments, annual permit renewal, mobile food establishments, and temporary food events.
section_ref: Environmental Health; food service establishment applications

title: Town of Christiansburg Planning and Zoning Overview
excerpt: Christiansburg Planning administers the zoning ordinance, supports the Planning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals, and assists with building permits, site plan review, subdivision plats, sign permits, and zoning ordinance enforcement.
section_ref: Planning Department; zoning and development assistance

title: Town of Christiansburg Town Code and Zoning Map Direction
excerpt: Christiansburg directs applicants to Chapter 42, Zoning, of the Town Code for zoning regulations and to the Zoning Map or Town GeoWeb service to view zoning designations for a particular property.

title: Town of Christiansburg GIS and Zoning Map
excerpt: Christiansburg directs users to the official zoning map and Town GeoWeb service to view zoning designations for a property before consulting Chapter 42 zoning standards.

title: Town of Christiansburg Permit and Plans Applications
excerpt: Christiansburg states that permit applications are used to issue permits that allow a project to begin, while plan applications typically result in approval or denial of a plan through the town's digital plans and permitting system.

title: Virginia Department of Health Food Establishment Plan Review
excerpt: Virginia Department of Health guidance directs new or remodeled foodservice facilities to submit plan review materials, apply for a food establishment permit before opening, and obtain inspection before permit issuance.

title: City of Roanoke Planning, Zoning, and Permitting
excerpt: Roanoke states that zoning laws determine permissible business types in certain areas, that construction projects require permits, and that applicants should contact Planning, Building, and Development before purchasing property or starting substantial upgrades.

title: City of Roanoke Zoning Code and Official Zoning Map
excerpt: Roanoke identifies Chapter 36.2 of the City Code as the zoning code and explains that the Official Zoning Map is available through the City's Real Estate GIS with Planning and Zoning layers for base and overlay zones.

title: City of Roanoke Business License Zoning Verification
excerpt: Roanoke requires zoning verification as part of the Commissioner of the Revenue business license process and recommends business owners check property zoning before signing a lease or purchasing property.
section_ref: Zoning Verification for a Business License

title: City of Roanoke ArcGIS Zoning Map Service
excerpt: The City of Roanoke ArcGIS REST zoning service identifies the public zoning map layer, service description, query support, and City of Roanoke copyright metadata for zoning map retrieval.

title: City of Roanoke Inspections and Permit Management
excerpt: Roanoke states most permits require at least one inspection and directs permit holders to manage inspections through eTRAKiT, with inspection requests scheduled by the next business day when submitted before the daily cutoff.

title: Roanoke County Zoning Office
excerpt: Roanoke County Planning and Zoning administers the zoning ordinance and land use codes, responds to land use concerns, and provides zoning-related public guidance for properties in the county.

title: Roanoke County Zoning Ordinance
excerpt: The Roanoke County Code identifies the county zoning ordinance and official zoning map as the framework for zoning districts, map amendments, special use permits, variances, and other zoning reviews.

title: Roanoke County Planning Office
excerpt: Roanoke County Planning provides land use and development guidance and works with zoning, development review, and county planning processes.

title: Roanoke County GIS Services and Zoning Details
excerpt: Roanoke County GIS applications provide web-based map access for county and Town of Vinton users, including property information and zoning details through GIS applications.

title: Roanoke County Building Safety and USBC Enforcement
excerpt: Roanoke County Building Safety enforces the Virginia Uniform Statewide Building Code and requires permits before construction, reconstruction, alteration, repair, conversion, or demolition of regulated buildings or structures.
